back_translation: For the choirmaster. A Maskil of the sons of Korah.
O God, we have heard with our ears; our fathers have told us the work You did in their days, in the days of old.
You drove out the nations and planted our fathers in their place; You crushed the peoples and made our fathers spread out — all this by Your hand.
For it was not by their sword that they took the land, nor did their arm save them; but it was Your right hand, Your arm, and the light of Your face, because You favored them.
O God, You are my King, who ordains victories for Jacob.
Through You we push down our foes; through Your name we trample those who rise against us.
For I do not trust in my bow, and my sword does not save me.
But You save us from our enemies, and You put to shame those who hate us.
In God we boast all day long, and we will praise Your name forever. Selah
But now You have rejected us and humbled us; You no longer go out with our armies.
You make us retreat from the foe, and those who hate us plunder us at will.
You give us up like sheep to be eaten, and You scatter us among the nations.
You sell Your people for a pittance, gaining nothing from their sale.
You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
You make us a byword among the nations, an object of head-shaking among the peoples.
All day long my disgrace is before me, and shame covers my face,
at the voice of the one who taunts and reviles, because of the enemy and the avenger.
All this has come upon us, though we have not forgotten You or betrayed Your covenant.
Our heart has not turned back, and our steps have not strayed from Your path.
But You have crushed us in the haunt of jackals and covered us with deep darkness.
If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a foreign god,
would not God discover this, since He knows the secrets of the heart?
But for Your sake we are killed all day long; we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered.
Awake, O Lord! Why do You sleep? Rise up! Do not reject us forever.
Why do You forget our affliction and oppression, and hide Your face from us?
For our soul has sunk down to the dust; our body clings to the earth.
Rise up and help us; redeem us for the sake of Your steadfast love.
